description:
Generate a standalone print-ready PDF of the 'scan to book' QR code, derived from the existing print/sheet.pdf template (the one with the green square). Steps:
1. Extract/identify the 'scan to book' QR area from print/sheet.pdf — reuse that visual style (green square, teal QR on dark bg).
2. Generate a new QR code using the existing lib/qr tooling encoding https://trilliummassage.la/redirect/screen_{hash} where {hash} is a new 8-char random hex.
3. Add the redirect entry to redirects.jsonl pointing to https://trilliummassage.la/blog/airbnb-host-promo-2026-03.
4. Lay it out as a single-page PDF at maximum printable size — fill the page with just the QR, leaving exactly 0.5in margins on all sides. Standard letter (8.5x11) or closest printable dimension is fine.
5. Save to print/screen_{hash}.pdf
6. Commit everything.
If anything about the PDF template structure or QR extraction is unclear, mail mayor/ with subject 'QUESTION: screen QR PDF' before proceeding.
notes: FIXED: scale23x container fetch was broken because Google Calendar API q= search can't tokenize 'scale23x__EVENT__'. Added blockingScope: 'general' which fetches all events then filters locally. Container data now appears on /display page. Committed 48f16ec, pushed.
title: Generate screen_{hash} QR code at max printable size

description:
Extend the redirect system beyond static redirects.jsonl to support mutable host-specific slugs without redeploy.

ARCHITECTURE:
- Static slugs (event handbills, promo pages): stay in redirects.jsonl → Next.js async redirects() — zero runtime cost
- Host-specific mutable slugs (host_*): stored in Vercel Edge Config (globally replicated KV, ~1ms lookups)

WHY EDGE CONFIG OVER SUPABASE:
- Supabase lookup = 50-200ms per redirect hit — unacceptable latency
- Edge Config = ~1ms globally replicated reads, updated via API without redeploy
- Perfect for redirect tables: high read frequency, low write frequency

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Vercel Edge Config store for host_* slug → destination URL mappings
2. Next.js middleware intercepts /redirect/host_* paths, looks up Edge Config, issues 307
3. Admin API endpoint to register/update host slugs (updates Edge Config via Vercel API)
4. Static redirects.jsonl unchanged — no migration needed

HOST FLOW:
- We generate QR → /redirect/host_{hash}
- Register hash in Edge Config: host_{hash} → /book?address=123+Main+St&host=Jane
- Host puts 1-pager in Airbnb guest booklet
- Guest scans → ~1ms lookup → lands on pre-filled booking form
- Host moves? Update Edge Config via admin API. QR unchanged.

FUTURE SCOPE ROUTING:
- airbnb_* → static (event promos, rarely change)
- handbill_* → static (printed materials)
- host_* → Edge Config (mutable, host address/info)
title: Dynamic redirect resolution via Vercel Edge Config for host-specific slugs

description:
attached_molecule: me-wisp-36nuw7
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-05T09:32:10Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Port QR Monkey API integration from ~/code/meet/qr/qr/ into lib/qr/. Create:

1. lib/qr/api.ts — fetchQRCode() hitting https://api.qrcode-monkey.com/qr/custom (no auth needed, POST JSON). Port qrCodeObject config: body=round, eye=frame2, eyeBall=ball2, bodyColor=#2dd4bf, bgColor=#1f1f1f, eyeBallColor=#ffbd4a (golden), size=2000, file=svg.

2. scripts/generate-qr-golden.ts — CLI script that calls the API for the default preset and saves to lib/qr/__tests__/fixtures/qr-default.svg. This is the SOURCE OF TRUTH.

3. lib/qr/generate.ts (LOCAL option) — uses qr-code-styling to produce the exact same visual output as QR Monkey. Every shape, dot style, eye frame style, eye ball style must be pixel-identical (or as close as possible) to the API SVG.

4. Update lib/qr/__tests__/generate.test.ts — golden fixtures MUST be regenerated by calling the API script (not local lib). The test proves: local generation == API generation for any given URL. If shapes differ, the local code needs adjustment until they match.

5. The URL pattern: https://trilliummassage.la/redirect/{scope}_{hash} e.g. /redirect/airbnb_host_promo_2026-03. The existing generate-qr.ts CLI already supports this URL pattern.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: The local output SVG and the API output SVG for the same URL must encode the same QR data and use the same visual style (body=round/rounded, eye frame=extra-rounded, eye ball=dot/ball). Shape-by-shape match is the goal. If qr-code-styling cannot replicate a specific QR Monkey shape exactly, document which shape params are closest.

Reference: /Users/trilliumsmith/code/meet/qr/qr/src/lib/fetchQRCode.ts
notes:
Implementation complete:

1. lib/qr/api.ts - fetchQRCode() hitting QR Monkey API (POST JSON, downloads SVG from imageUrl)
2. scripts/generate-qr-golden.ts - generates golden fixtures for 5 URLs × 3 presets = 15 SVGs
3. lib/qr/__tests__/generate.test.ts - 33 tests: regression (stability), style matching (API vs local colors), structural checks
4. lib/qr/index.ts - exports fetchQRCode

Key findings:
- QR Monkey API uses RGB color notation (rgb(45,212,191)), local qr-code-styling uses hex (#2dd4bf) — color comparison test handles both formats
- Local qr-code-styling generates non-deterministic clip-path IDs (numeric suffix varies per run) — normalization regex handles this
- Exact SVG string match between API and local is impossible (different rendering engines, different SVG structures) — tests verify color equivalence instead
- Shape mapping: body=round→dotsOptions.type=rounded, eye=frame2→cornersSquareOptions.type=extra-rounded, eyeBall=ball2→cornersDotOptions.type=dot (already configured correctly in existing config.ts)
title: Port QR Monkey API + golden fixture generation + local matching

description: Vercel build logs show: '@sentry/nextjs - Client] Warning: No auth token provided. Will not create release.' The withSentryConfig call in next.config.js needs authToken set via SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN env var in Vercel project settings. Token can be generated at https://sentry.io/settings/auth-tokens/ with project:releases scope. Then add SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN to Vercel environment variables.
title: Add Sentry authToken to suppress release-creation warning

description:
Root cause investigation (2026-03-02):

Single Dolt server running: pid 97397, port 3307, data-dir=/Users/trilliumsmith/gt/.dolt-data/

Databases served: beads, beads_mel, gt, life, massage, massage_tracker, mel, ops, personal, tr, witness. MISSING: 'gastown'.

The /code/gastown source repo .beads/config.yaml uses sync.mode=dolt-native. bd derives db name from repo dir ('gastown') and tries to connect at 127.0.0.1:3307 — db does not exist.

Key ambiguity: rig prefix is 'gt' (gastown/config.json) and a 'gt' db EXISTS in .dolt-data/. Unclear if source repo beads should point at 'gt' (naming mismatch) or needs its own 'gastown' db.

Broader risk: other rigs may have similar mismatches — silently failing or writing to wrong dbs. Audit all rigs.

Secondary: git hooks in /code/gastown all at v0.0.0 (current 0.57.0) — run 'bd hooks install --force' after db fixed.

Fix options:
  1. bd init in /code/gastown to create 'gastown' db in Dolt
  2. Reconfigure source repo .beads to use 'gt' db explicitly
  3. Audit all rigs for db name mismatches
  4. bd hooks install --force in /code/gastown
title: Dolt beads misconfiguration: 'gastown' db missing, hooks outdated, possible db naming mismatch

   Check Witness and Refinery health for each rig.

   Maintain daemon logs and state files.

- Check for completed callback wisps

22. **[open]** Resolve external dependencies

24. **[open]** Refresh heartbeat
   Signal liveness to the daemon.

25. **[open]** Aggregate daily patrol digests

description:
Root cause investigation (2026-03-02):

Single Dolt server running: pid 97397, port 3307, data-dir=/Users/trilliumsmith/gt/.dolt-data/

Databases actually served: beads, beads_mel, gt, life, massage, massage_tracker, mel, ops, personal, tr, witness

MISSING: 'gastown' database — never initialized in Dolt.

The /code/gastown source repo .beads/config.yaml uses sync.mode=dolt-native. bd derives the database name from the repo directory name ('gastown') and tries to connect to it at 127.0.0.1:3307 — that database does not exist.

Key ambiguity: the rig prefix is 'gt' (gastown/config.json) and a 'gt' database DOES exist in .dolt-data/. It is unclear whether:
  (a) The source repo beads should point at the 'gt' database (naming mismatch / misconfiguration), OR
  (b) A separate 'gastown' database needs to be created for the source repo's own beads

Broader risk: there may be other rigs with similar mismatches between expected db names and what Dolt serves — repos silently failing or writing to wrong databases. Audit recommended.

Secondary issue: git hooks in /code/gastown are all at v0.0.0, current is 0.57.0 — need 'bd hooks install --force' after db is fixed.

Fix candidates:

description:
## Molecule Execution Summary

**Molecule**: mol-deacon-patrol
**Steps**: 25

**Completed**: 0/25

---

### Steps

1. **[open]** Execute registered plugins
   Execute registered plugins.

2. **[open]** Aggregate daily costs [DISABLED]
   **DISABLED** - Skip this step entirely.

3. **[open]** Mid-cycle heartbeat refresh
   Refresh the heartbeat mid-cycle to prevent the daemon from killing us during long patrols.

4. **[open]** Refresh heartbeat
   Signal liveness to the daemon.

5. **[open]** End-of-cycle inbox hygiene
   Verify inbox hygiene before ending patrol cycle.

6. **[open]** Dispatch molecules with resolved gates
   Find molecules blocked on gates that have now closed and dispatch them.

7. **[open]** Compact expired wisps
   Run TTL-based wisp compaction to manage storage growth.

8. **[open]** Check Witness and Refinery health
   Check Witness and Refinery health for each rig.

9. **[open]** Maintain dog pool
   Ensure dog pool has available workers for dispatch.

10. **[open]** Check for stuck dogs
   Check for dogs that have been working too long (stuck).

11. **[open]** Detect abandoned work
   **DETECT ONLY** - Check for orphaned state and dispatch to dog if found.

12. **[open]** Detect zombie polecats (NO KILL AUTHORITY)
   Defense-in-depth DETECTION of zombie polecats that Witness should have cleaned.

13. **[open]** Clean up orphaned claude subagent processes
   Clean up orphaned claude subagent processes.

14. **[open]** Detect and clean runtime test pollution
   Detect and clean runtime pollution left by tests and dead processes.

15. **[open]** Loop or exit for respawn
   End of patrol cycle decision.

16. **[open]** Rotate logs and prune state
   Maintain daemon logs and state files.

17. **[open]** Fire notifications
   Fire notifications for convoy and cross-rig events.

18. **[open]** Check convoy completion
   Check convoy completion status.

19. **[open]** Evaluate pending async gates
   Evaluate pending async gates.

20. **[open]** Handle callbacks from agents
   First, clean up wisps from previous cycles (closed wisps + abandoned wisps):
- Check for completed callback wisps
- Process any pending agent responses
- Archive or discard stale wisps

21. **[open]** Aggregate daily patrol digests
   **DAILY DIGEST** - Aggregate yesterday's patrol cycle digests.

22. **[open]** Resolve external dependencies
   Resolve external dependencies across rigs.

23. **[open]** Detect cleanup needs
   **DETECT ONLY** - Check if cleanup is needed and dispatch to dog.

24. **[open]** Send compaction digest report
   Generate and send the daily compaction digest.

25. **[open]** Check own context limit
   Check own context limit.
title: Digest: mol-deacon-patrol

description:
Root cause investigation (2026-03-02):

Single Dolt server running: pid 97397, port 3307, data-dir=/Users/trilliumsmith/gt/.dolt-data/

Databases actually served: beads, beads_mel, gt, life, massage, massage_tracker, mel, ops, personal, tr, witness. MISSING: 'gastown'.

The /code/gastown source repo .beads/config.yaml uses sync.mode=dolt-native. bd derives the db name from the repo directory ('gastown') and tries to connect at 127.0.0.1:3307 — db does not exist.

Key ambiguity: rig prefix is 'gt' (gastown/config.json) and a 'gt' db EXISTS in .dolt-data/. Unclear if source repo beads should point at 'gt' (naming mismatch) or needs its own 'gastown' db.

Broader risk: other rigs may have similar mismatches — silently failing or writing to wrong databases. Audit all rigs.

Secondary: git hooks in /code/gastown all at v0.0.0, current 0.57.0 — run 'bd hooks install --force' after db fixed.

Fix options:
  1. bd init in /code/gastown to create 'gastown' db in Dolt
  2. Reconfigure source repo .beads to use 'gt' db explicitly
  3. Audit all rigs for db name mismatches
  4. bd hooks install --force in /code/gastown
title: Dolt beads misconfiguration: 'gastown' db missing, hooks outdated, possible db naming mismatch

description:
CRITICAL SYSTEM-LEVEL ISSUE:

**Timeline:**
- 00:43 - GT binary update triggered deadlock
- 03:58 - Last successful patrol
- 01:26 - Binary rebuild attempted
- Current: 77 gt processes stuck in UE (uninterruptible sleep) state

**Symptoms:**
- All gt commands that write to /private/tmp/claude-501 hang indefinitely
- Processes cannot be killed (kernel-level blocking)
- Filesystem appears to have I/O deadlock on task output files
- Daemon restart failed

**Impact:**
- Talon Witness blocked (cannot complete polling cycles)
- All patrol work blocked
- Any command accessing task output directory hangs
- Only read-only operations (gt hook, gt mail) work

**Root Cause:** Filesystem deadlock on I/O, likely kernel-level issue or tmpfs problem

**Required Action:**
- System reboot OR
- Kernel I/O investigation
- This is beyond agent remediation scope - requires human system administration

**Blocking:** All patrol work, all witness operations, all daemon operations
title: 🚨 P0 ESCALATION: GT Binary Deadlock - UE Process Hang

description:
User confirmed neighbor shapes are failing — some shapes are misoriented or not joining correctly with adjacent zones. Need two things:

1. Unit test for all 16 TRBL adjacency masks: render each shape key and visually confirm correct orientation
2. Visual SVG grid test for this specific 4x4 pattern:
   0 0 0 0
   0 1 1 1
   0 1 0 0
   0 0 0 0
   Should connect horizontally (row 1: right-joining) and vertically (col 1: bottom-joining) correctly.

Located in scripts/generate-qr-native.ts SHAPES lookup table and renderDots().
The SHAPES dict maps TRBL bitmasks to SVG path content. Suspect some paths have wrong orientation relative to adjacency assumption (T=top, R=right, B=bottom, L=left).
title: Fix adjacency shape orientation + add grid connection tests

description:
When bd connects to Dolt, it currently reads only from .beads/metadata.json (dolt_server_host / dolt_server_port). In a multi-machine Gas Town setup, every rig on every machine has a hardcoded host that drifts when Dolt moves.

GT already sets GT_DOLT_HOST and GT_DOLT_PORT env vars for all agents. bd should check these first, so the town-level env config becomes the single source of truth.

Resolution order (highest to lowest):
1. CLI flag
2. BD_DOLT_HOST / BD_DOLT_PORT env vars
3. GT_DOLT_HOST / GT_DOLT_PORT env vars (already set by gt on all agents)
4. config.yaml dolt.host / dolt.port
5. metadata.json (current only source)

Prevents the recurring host-drift failure pattern in multi-machine deployments.
title: Respect GT_DOLT_HOST / GT_DOLT_PORT env vars as default Dolt connection

description:
Reap stale wisps and close stale issues across all Dolt databases.

The Reaper Dog executes this formula to clean up wisp and issue tables.
SQL operations are exposed via `gt reaper` CLI helpers — the Dog calls
these commands, inspects results, and uses judgment for anomalies.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure cleanup work:
1. Scan all production databases for candidates
2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper

description:
Stage, commit, and push JSONL files to the archive repository.

**1. Stage changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> add -A *.jsonl */
```

**2. Check for changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> diff --cached --quiet
# If no changes, skip commit
```

**3. Commit with counts:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> commit -m "backup <timestamp>: <db>=<count> ..." --author="Gas Town Daemon <daemon@gastown.local>"
```

Include failed databases in commit message so staleness is visible.

**4. Push to origin:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> push origin main
```

**5. Handle push failures:**
Track consecutive failures. After 3 consecutive failures,
escalate to Mayor.

**Exit criteria:** Changes committed and pushed (or no changes to commit).
title: Commit and push to git archive

description:
Sync Dolt database backups to local and offsite storage.

The Backup Dog runs `dolt backup sync` for each production database, then
rsyncs the local backup directory to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

Current behavior (from dolt_backup.go):
- Discovers databases with backup remotes configured
- Runs `dolt backup sync <name>-backup` per database
- Rsyncs .dolt-backup/ to iCloud Drive

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Sync each production database to its backup remote
2. Rsync local backups to offsite (iCloud Drive)
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to back up (or auto-discover) |

## Safety

Backups are additive — they never modify production data. Backup sync
overwrites the backup destination, but that is the intended behavior.
title: mol-dog-backup

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

description:
Probe Dolt server health and inspect resource conditions.

The Doctor Dog performs comprehensive health checks on the Dolt SQL server:
connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.
title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Export Dolt databases to JSONL and push to git archive.

The JSONL Dog exports each production database's issues table (scrubbed of
ephemeral data) plus supplemental tables to JSONL files, commits them to a
git repository, and pushes to origin. This provides a human-readable,
git-versioned backup of all durable work product.

Current behavior (from jsonl_git_backup.go):
- Exports issues table with scrub filter (excludes messages, events, wisps, etc.)
- Exports supplemental tables (comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata)
- Writes per-database subdirectories + legacy flat files
- Commits with counts and pushes to git remote
- Escalates after 3 consecutive push failures

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Export each database to JSONL files (with scrub)
2. Commit and push to git archive
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to export |
| scrub | config | Whether to filter ephemeral data (default true) |

## Safety

Exports are read-only against Dolt. Git operations are append-only
(commit + push). The archive repo is a separate repository from
the main codebase.
title: mol-dog-jsonl

description:
Run dolt backup sync for each production database.

**1. Determine databases:**
Use configured databases list, or auto-discover databases with
`<name>-backup` remotes configured.

**2. For each database:**
```bash
cd <data_dir>/<db>
dolt backup sync <db>-backup
```

**3. Record results:**
- Databases synced successfully
- Databases that failed (with error)
- Duration per database

**Exit criteria:** All databases attempted, results recorded.
title: Sync databases to backup remotes

description:
Stage, commit, and push JSONL files to the archive repository.

**1. Stage changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> add -A *.jsonl */
```

**2. Check for changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> diff --cached --quiet
# If no changes, skip commit
```

**3. Commit with counts:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> commit -m "backup <timestamp>: <db>=<count> ..." --author="Gas Town Daemon <daemon@gastown.local>"
```

Include failed databases in commit message so staleness is visible.

**4. Push to origin:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> push origin main
```

**5. Handle push failures:**
Track consecutive failures. After 3 consecutive failures,
escalate to Mayor.

**Exit criteria:** Changes committed and pushed (or no changes to commit).
title: Commit and push to git archive

description:
Discover databases and count candidates for each operation.

**1. List databases to scan:**
```bash
gt reaper databases --json
```
Or use configured database list from  variable.

**2. Scan each database:**
```bash
gt reaper scan --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --max-age=24h0m0s --purge-age=168h0m0s \
  --mail-age=168h0m0s --stale-age=720h0m0s \
  --json
```

**3. Inspect results for anomalies:**
- Check the `anomalies` array in the JSON output
- Flag any `dangling_parent_ref` anomalies — these indicate wisps with
  parent dependency records pointing to purged/missing parents
- If `open_wisps` exceeds 500, escalate

**4. Decide whether to proceed:**
- If no candidates found across all databases, skip remaining steps
- If anomalies are severe, consider escalating before proceeding

**Exit criteria:** Scan complete, candidates identified, anomalies flagged.
title: Scan databases for reaper candidates

description:
Close issues that have been open with no status change past stale_issue_age.

**1. For each database with stale candidates:**
```bash
gt reaper auto-close --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --stale-age=720h0m0s \
  {{#if dry_run}}--dry-run{{/if}} --json
```

**2. Inspect results:**
- Review the count of closed issues
- Verify exclusions are working (P0/P1, epics, dependency-linked issues
  should never be auto-closed)

**Exit criteria:** All eligible stale issues auto-closed.
title: Auto-close stale issues

description:
Verify export integrity before committing.

**1. Filter test pollution:**
Remove records matching test patterns from exported JSONL:
- Titles starting with "Test Issue" or "test_"
- IDs matching test patterns: bd-1, bd-abc12, testdb_*, beads_t*, etc.

```go
applyPollutionFilter(gitRepo, databases)
```

**2. Spike detection:**
Compare current export record counts against previous commit:
```go
verifyExportCounts(gitRepo, databases, counts, threshold)
```

**3. Evaluate results:**
- If delta > 0.20 (default 20%) for ANY database → HALT
- Log anomalies: sudden jumps = pollution, sudden drops = data loss
- Escalate to Mayor with spike report
- Do NOT proceed to commit/push

**4. First export:**
Skip verification when no baseline exists (previous commit has no file).

**Exit criteria:** All databases within threshold, or halted with escalation.
title: Verify export counts and filter pollution

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

description:
Reap stale wisps and close stale issues across all Dolt databases.

The Reaper Dog executes this formula to clean up wisp and issue tables.
SQL operations are exposed via `gt reaper` CLI helpers — the Dog calls
these commands, inspects results, and uses judgment for anomalies.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure cleanup work:
1. Scan all production databases for candidates
2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper

description:
Sync Dolt database backups to local and offsite storage.

The Backup Dog runs `dolt backup sync` for each production database, then
rsyncs the local backup directory to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

Current behavior (from dolt_backup.go):
- Discovers databases with backup remotes configured
- Runs `dolt backup sync <name>-backup` per database
- Rsyncs .dolt-backup/ to iCloud Drive

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Sync each production database to its backup remote
2. Rsync local backups to offsite (iCloud Drive)
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to back up (or auto-discover) |

## Safety

Backups are additive — they never modify production data. Backup sync
overwrites the backup destination, but that is the intended behavior.
title: mol-dog-backup

description:
Check basic server health and measure latency.

**1. Connectivity check:**
```bash
dolt sql -q "SELECT active_branch()"
```
Use active_branch() — lightweight probe that won't block behind queued queries
(per Dolt CEO recommendation).

**2. Measure latency:**
Time the probe query. Warn if > 5000ms.

**3. Record results:**
- Server reachable: yes/no
- Latency: <duration>
- Warning: if latency exceeds threshold

**If server unreachable:**
This is a critical failure. Escalate immediately:
```bash
gt escalate -s CRITICAL "Dolt: server unreachable on port 3307"
```

**Exit criteria:** Server connectivity confirmed (or escalated).
title: Probe Dolt server connectivity

description:
Probe Dolt server health and inspect resource conditions.

The Doctor Dog performs comprehensive health checks on the Dolt SQL server:
connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.

```bash
rsync -a --delete .dolt-backup/ ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/gt-dolt-backup/
```

- Success/failure
- Duration

**Note:** This is a stopgap until proper dolt remote push is configured.
If iCloud is unavailable, log and continue — not a fatal error.

**Exit criteria:** Offsite sync attempted.

2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper

description:
Mayor's daemon patrol loop.

The Deacon is the Mayor's background process that runs continuously, handling callbacks, monitoring rig health, and performing cleanup. Each patrol cycle runs these steps in sequence, then loops or exits.

## Idle Town Principle

**The Deacon should be silent/invisible when the town is healthy and idle.**

- Skip HEALTH_CHECK nudges when no active work exists
- Sleep 60+ seconds between patrol cycles (longer when idle)
- Let the feed subscription wake agents on actual events
- The daemon (10-minute heartbeat) is the safety net for dead sessions

This prevents flooding idle agents with health checks every few seconds.

## Mayor Nudge Policy

**NEVER nudge the Mayor for health checks, pings, or routine status.**
The Mayor should ONLY be nudged for genuine emergencies:
- Escalations that require Mayor decision-making
- Sending mail to mayor/ (which is non-interruptive)

Health pings to the Mayor are a ZFC violation — they burn Mayor context
on ceremony instead of work. The Mayor is your boss, not a subordinate
to be monitored. Use `gt mail send mayor/` for non-urgent communication.

## Second-Order Monitoring

Witnesses passively monitor Deacon health by checking the Deacon's agent bead
last_activity timestamp. This prevents the "who watches the watchers" problem —
if the Deacon dies, Witnesses detect it and escalate to the Mayor.

No WITNESS_PING mail is sent. Witnesses observe the Deacon's last_activity
timestamp instead, and only send an alert to the Mayor if the Deacon appears
unresponsive (>5 minutes stale). This avoids heartbeat mail spam.
title: mol-deacon-patrol

description:
Sync Dolt database backups to local and offsite storage.

The Backup Dog runs `dolt backup sync` for each production database, then
rsyncs the local backup directory to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

Current behavior (from dolt_backup.go):
- Discovers databases with backup remotes configured
- Runs `dolt backup sync <name>-backup` per database
- Rsyncs .dolt-backup/ to iCloud Drive

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Sync each production database to its backup remote
2. Rsync local backups to offsite (iCloud Drive)
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to back up (or auto-discover) |

## Safety

Backups are additive — they never modify production data. Backup sync
overwrites the backup destination, but that is the intended behavior.
title: mol-dog-backup

description:
Rsync local backups to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

**1. Check prerequisites:**
- .dolt-backup/ directory exists
- iCloud Drive path is accessible

**2. Run offsite sync:**
```bash
rsync -a --delete .dolt-backup/ ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/gt-dolt-backup/
```

**3. Record results:**
- Success/failure
- Duration

**Note:** This is a stopgap until proper dolt remote push is configured.
If iCloud is unavailable, log and continue — not a fatal error.

**Exit criteria:** Offsite sync attempted.
title: Sync backups to offsite storage

description:
Delete closed wisps past purge_age and closed mail past mail_delete_age.

**1. For each database with purge candidates:**
```bash
gt reaper purge --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --purge-age=168h0m0s --mail-age=168h0m0s \
  {{#if dry_run}}--dry-run{{/if}} --json
```

**2. Inspect results for anomalies:**
- Check for `dolt_commit_failed` anomalies — purged data may not be persisted
- Verify purge counts are reasonable (not suspiciously high or low)

**Safety:** Only deletes wisps that are already closed AND past the purge
retention window. Active wisps are never touched. Reverse dependency
references are cleaned up to prevent dangling parent refs.

**Exit criteria:** Old closed wisps and mail purged.
title: Purge old closed wisps and mail

description:
Ensure ALL implementation work is committed before cleanup.

**CRITICAL: You MUST commit all changes from implementation.**
NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.
ALWAYS commit ALL uncommitted changes from your implementation.

**1. Check for uncommitted changes:**
```bash
git status
```

**2. If there are ANY uncommitted changes, commit them now:**
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <descriptive message> (massage-02g)"
```

**3. If working tree is already clean, that's fine — but you MUST still have commits (step 4).**

**4. VERIFY commits exist (HARD GATE — do NOT close this step without passing):**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```

This MUST show at least 1 commit. If it shows NOTHING:
- You have NOT completed your implementation. Do NOT close this step.
- Go back to the implement step and do the work.
- If the task genuinely requires no code changes (already fixed upstream, etc.),
  run `gt done --status DEFERRED` and skip remaining steps.
- Do NOT proceed to cleanup or submit with zero commits.

**5. VERIFY clean working tree:**
```bash
git status
```
Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".

**Report-only tasks (audits, reviews, research — no code changes):**
If your task produced no code changes, verify your findings are persisted to the bead:
```bash
bd show massage-02g    # Check notes/design fields have your findings
```
If findings are persisted, proceed to cleanup. `gt done --cleanup-status clean`
handles the no-commit case for report-only tasks.

**Exit criteria:** Working tree clean AND either (a) at least 1 commit ahead of origin/main, or (b) report-only task with findings persisted to bead.
title: Commit all implementation changes

description:
Probe Dolt server health and inspect resource conditions.

The Doctor Dog performs comprehensive health checks on the Dolt SQL server:
connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.
title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

description:
Discover databases and count candidates for each operation.

**1. List databases to scan:**
```bash
gt reaper databases --json
```
Or use configured database list from  variable.

**2. Scan each database:**
```bash
gt reaper scan --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --max-age=24h0m0s --purge-age=168h0m0s \
  --mail-age=168h0m0s --stale-age=720h0m0s \
  --json
```

**3. Inspect results for anomalies:**
- Check the `anomalies` array in the JSON output
- Flag any `dangling_parent_ref` anomalies — these indicate wisps with
  parent dependency records pointing to purged/missing parents
- If `open_wisps` exceeds 500, escalate

**4. Decide whether to proceed:**
- If no candidates found across all databases, skip remaining steps
- If anomalies are severe, consider escalating before proceeding

**Exit criteria:** Scan complete, candidates identified, anomalies flagged.
title: Scan databases for reaper candidates

description:
Export Dolt databases to JSONL and push to git archive.

The JSONL Dog exports each production database's issues table (scrubbed of
ephemeral data) plus supplemental tables to JSONL files, commits them to a
git repository, and pushes to origin. This provides a human-readable,
git-versioned backup of all durable work product.

Current behavior (from jsonl_git_backup.go):
- Exports issues table with scrub filter (excludes messages, events, wisps, etc.)
- Exports supplemental tables (comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata)
- Writes per-database subdirectories + legacy flat files
- Commits with counts and pushes to git remote
- Escalates after 3 consecutive push failures

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Export each database to JSONL files (with scrub)
2. Commit and push to git archive
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to export |
| scrub | config | Whether to filter ephemeral data (default true) |

## Safety

Exports are read-only against Dolt. Git operations are append-only
(commit + push). The archive repo is a separate repository from
the main codebase.
title: mol-dog-jsonl

- Exports supplemental tables (comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata)
- Writes per-database subdirectories + legacy flat files
- Commits with counts and pushes to git remote
- Escalates after 3 consecutive push failures

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Export each database to JSONL files (with scrub)
2. Commit and push to git archive
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to export |
| scrub | config | Whether to filter ephemeral data (default true) |

## Safety

Exports are read-only against Dolt. Git operations are append-only
(commit + push). The archive repo is a separate repository from
the main codebase.
title: mol-dog-jsonl

description:
Reap stale wisps and close stale issues across all Dolt databases.

The Reaper Dog executes this formula to clean up wisp and issue tables.
SQL operations are exposed via `gt reaper` CLI helpers — the Dog calls
these commands, inspects results, and uses judgment for anomalies.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure cleanup work:
1. Scan all production databases for candidates
2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper

description:

connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan

5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)

4. Return to kennel

|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.

title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

```bash
dolt sql -q "SELECT active_branch()"
```
Use active_branch() — lightweight probe that won't block behind queued queries
(per Dolt CEO recommendation).

**2. Measure latency:**
Time the probe query. Warn if > 5000ms.

**3. Record results:**
- Server reachable: yes/no
- Latency: <duration>
- Warning: if latency exceeds threshold

**If server unreachable:**
This is a critical failure. Escalate immediately:
```bash
gt escalate -s CRITICAL "Dolt: server unreachable on port 3307"
```

**Exit criteria:** Server connectivity confirmed (or escalated).
title: Probe Dolt server connectivity

description:
Export Dolt databases to JSONL and push to git archive.

The JSONL Dog exports each production database's issues table (scrubbed of
ephemeral data) plus supplemental tables to JSONL files, commits them to a
git repository, and pushes to origin. This provides a human-readable,
git-versioned backup of all durable work product.

Current behavior (from jsonl_git_backup.go):
- Exports issues table with scrub filter (excludes messages, events, wisps, etc.)
- Exports supplemental tables (comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata)
- Writes per-database subdirectories + legacy flat files
- Commits with counts and pushes to git remote
- Escalates after 3 consecutive push failures

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Export each database to JSONL files (with scrub)
2. Commit and push to git archive
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to export |
| scrub | config | Whether to filter ephemeral data (default true) |

## Safety

Exports are read-only against Dolt. Git operations are append-only
(commit + push). The archive repo is a separate repository from
the main codebase.
title: mol-dog-jsonl

description:
Stage, commit, and push JSONL files to the archive repository.

**1. Stage changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> add -A *.jsonl */
```

**2. Check for changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> diff --cached --quiet
# If no changes, skip commit
```

**3. Commit with counts:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> commit -m "backup <timestamp>: <db>=<count> ..." --author="Gas Town Daemon <daemon@gastown.local>"
```

Include failed databases in commit message so staleness is visible.

**4. Push to origin:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> push origin main
```

**5. Handle push failures:**
Track consecutive failures. After 3 consecutive failures,
escalate to Mayor.

**Exit criteria:** Changes committed and pushed (or no changes to commit).
title: Commit and push to git archive

description:
Delete closed wisps past purge_age and closed mail past mail_delete_age.

**1. For each database with purge candidates:**
```bash
gt reaper purge --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --purge-age=168h0m0s --mail-age=168h0m0s \
  {{#if dry_run}}--dry-run{{/if}} --json
```

**2. Inspect results for anomalies:**
- Check for `dolt_commit_failed` anomalies — purged data may not be persisted
- Verify purge counts are reasonable (not suspiciously high or low)

**Safety:** Only deletes wisps that are already closed AND past the purge
retention window. Active wisps are never touched. Reverse dependency
references are cleaned up to prevent dangling parent refs.

**Exit criteria:** Old closed wisps and mail purged.
title: Purge old closed wisps and mail

description:
Discover databases and count candidates for each operation.

**1. List databases to scan:**
```bash
gt reaper databases --json
```
Or use configured database list from  variable.

**2. Scan each database:**
```bash
gt reaper scan --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --max-age=24h0m0s --purge-age=168h0m0s \
  --mail-age=168h0m0s --stale-age=720h0m0s \
  --json
```

**3. Inspect results for anomalies:**
- Check the `anomalies` array in the JSON output
- Flag any `dangling_parent_ref` anomalies — these indicate wisps with
  parent dependency records pointing to purged/missing parents
- If `open_wisps` exceeds 500, escalate

**4. Decide whether to proceed:**
- If no candidates found across all databases, skip remaining steps
- If anomalies are severe, consider escalating before proceeding

**Exit criteria:** Scan complete, candidates identified, anomalies flagged.
title: Scan databases for reaper candidates

description: Deacon patrol cycle. Monitors agents, processes lifecycle events, maintains infrastructure health.
title: mol-deacon-patrol

description:
Sync Dolt database backups to local and offsite storage.

The Backup Dog runs `dolt backup sync` for each production database, then
rsyncs the local backup directory to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

Current behavior (from dolt_backup.go):
- Discovers databases with backup remotes configured
- Runs `dolt backup sync <name>-backup` per database
- Rsyncs .dolt-backup/ to iCloud Drive

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Sync each production database to its backup remote
2. Rsync local backups to offsite (iCloud Drive)
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to back up (or auto-discover) |

## Safety

Backups are additive — they never modify production data. Backup sync
overwrites the backup destination, but that is the intended behavior.

description:
Probe Dolt server health and inspect resource conditions.

The Doctor Dog performs comprehensive health checks on the Dolt SQL server:
connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.

title: mol-refinery-patrol

description:
Submit your work and clean up. You cease to exist after this step.

**Self-Cleaning Model:**
Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
1. Pushes your branch to origin
2. Creates an MR bead in the merge queue
3. Nukes your sandbox (worktree removal)
4. Exits your session immediately

**Pre-flight: Verify you have actual work to submit (HARD GATE):**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```
This MUST show at least 1 commit. If it shows nothing, do NOT run `gt done`.
`gt done` will reject zero-commit branches for polecats.

**Run gt done:**
```bash
# For code tasks with pre-verification (recommended — enables fast-path merge):
gt done --pre-verified

# For code tasks without pre-verification (gates skipped or failed):
gt done

# For report-only tasks (no commits — audits, reviews, research):
gt done --cleanup-status clean
```

You should see output like:
```
✓ Work submitted to merge queue
  MR ID: gt-xxxxx
  Source: polecat/<name>
  Target: main
  Issue: massage-02g
✓ Sandbox nuked
✓ Session exiting
```

**What happens next (not your concern):**
- Refinery batches MRs and runs gates on the merged stack
- If green: all MRs in the batch merge to main at once
- If red: Refinery bisects to isolate the culprit, merges the good ones
- Refinery fixes failures itself (inline or via helper polecats)
- Refinery closes your issue after successful merge

You are NOT involved in any of that. You're gone. Done means gone.

**Exit criteria:** Work submitted, sandbox nuked, session exited.
title: Submit work and self-clean

title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

title: Report findings and return to kennel

description:
Verify export integrity before committing.

**1. Filter test pollution:**
Remove records matching test patterns from exported JSONL:
- Titles starting with "Test Issue" or "test_"
- IDs matching test patterns: bd-1, bd-abc12, testdb_*, beads_t*, etc.

```go
applyPollutionFilter(gitRepo, databases)
```

**2. Spike detection:**
Compare current export record counts against previous commit:
```go
verifyExportCounts(gitRepo, databases, counts, threshold)
```

**3. Evaluate results:**
- If delta > 0.20 (default 20%) for ANY database → HALT
- Log anomalies: sudden jumps = pollution, sudden drops = data loss
- Escalate to Mayor with spike report
- Do NOT proceed to commit/push

**4. First export:**
Skip verification when no baseline exists (previous commit has no file).

**Exit criteria:** All databases within threshold, or halted with escalation.
title: Verify export counts and filter pollution

description:
Check basic server health and measure latency.

**1. Connectivity check:**
```bash
dolt sql -q "SELECT active_branch()"
```
Use active_branch() — lightweight probe that won't block behind queued queries
(per Dolt CEO recommendation).

**2. Measure latency:**
Time the probe query. Warn if > 5000ms.

**3. Record results:**
- Server reachable: yes/no
- Latency: <duration>
- Warning: if latency exceeds threshold

**If server unreachable:**
This is a critical failure. Escalate immediately:
```bash
gt escalate -s CRITICAL "Dolt: server unreachable on port 3307"
```

**Exit criteria:** Server connectivity confirmed (or escalated).
title: Probe Dolt server connectivity

description:
Export Dolt databases to JSONL and push to git archive.

The JSONL Dog exports each production database's issues table (scrubbed of
ephemeral data) plus supplemental tables to JSONL files, commits them to a
git repository, and pushes to origin. This provides a human-readable,
git-versioned backup of all durable work product.

Current behavior (from jsonl_git_backup.go):
- Exports issues table with scrub filter (excludes messages, events, wisps, etc.)
- Exports supplemental tables (comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata)
- Writes per-database subdirectories + legacy flat files
- Commits with counts and pushes to git remote
- Escalates after 3 consecutive push failures

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Export each database to JSONL files (with scrub)
2. Commit and push to git archive
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to export |
| scrub | config | Whether to filter ephemeral data (default true) |

## Safety

Exports are read-only against Dolt. Git operations are append-only
(commit + push). The archive repo is a separate repository from
the main codebase.
title: mol-dog-jsonl

description:
Reap stale wisps and close stale issues across all Dolt databases.

The Reaper Dog executes this formula to clean up wisp and issue tables.
SQL operations are exposed via `gt reaper` CLI helpers — the Dog calls
these commands, inspects results, and uses judgment for anomalies.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure cleanup work:
1. Scan all production databases for candidates
2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper

description:
Export each database's tables to JSONL files.

**1. Determine databases:**
Use configured databases list.

**2. For each database, export:**
- issues table (with scrub filter to exclude ephemeral data)
- supplemental tables: comments, config, dependencies, labels, metadata

**3. Write output:**
- Per-database subdirectory: `<git_repo>/<db>/issues.jsonl`, etc.
- Legacy flat file: `<git_repo>/<db>.jsonl`

**4. Record results:**
- Records exported per database per table
- Any export failures

```bash
dolt sql -r json -q "SELECT * FROM <db>.issues <scrub_filter>" > <output>
```

**Exit criteria:** All databases exported to JSONL.
title: Export databases to JSONL

description:
Stage, commit, and push JSONL files to the archive repository.

**1. Stage changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> add -A *.jsonl */
```

**2. Check for changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> diff --cached --quiet
# If no changes, skip commit
```

**3. Commit with counts:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> commit -m "backup <timestamp>: <db>=<count> ..." --author="Gas Town Daemon <daemon@gastown.local>"
```

Include failed databases in commit message so staleness is visible.

**4. Push to origin:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> push origin main
```

**5. Handle push failures:**
Track consecutive failures. After 3 consecutive failures,
escalate to Mayor.

**Exit criteria:** Changes committed and pushed (or no changes to commit).
title: Commit and push to git archive

description:
Close wisps past max_age whose parent molecule is closed or missing.

**1. For each database with reap candidates:**
```bash
gt reaper reap --db=<name> --port=3307 \
  --max-age=24h0m0s {{#if dry_run}}--dry-run{{/if}} --json
```

**2. Inspect results:**
- Verify reaped count matches scan expectations
- Check for any anomalies in the response

**3. Alert check:**
If total open wisps across all databases exceed 500,
log a warning and consider escalating.

**Exit criteria:** All stale wisps closed (or dry-run counts reported).
title: Reap stale wisps

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

description:
Probe Dolt server health and inspect resource conditions.

The Doctor Dog performs comprehensive health checks on the Dolt SQL server:
connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.
title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Verify export integrity before committing.

**1. Filter test pollution:**
Remove records matching test patterns from exported JSONL:
- Titles starting with "Test Issue" or "test_"
- IDs matching test patterns: bd-1, bd-abc12, testdb_*, beads_t*, etc.

```go
applyPollutionFilter(gitRepo, databases)
```

**2. Spike detection:**
Compare current export record counts against previous commit:
```go
verifyExportCounts(gitRepo, databases, counts, threshold)
```

**3. Evaluate results:**
- If delta > 0.20 (default 20%) for ANY database → HALT
- Log anomalies: sudden jumps = pollution, sudden drops = data loss
- Escalate to Mayor with spike report
- Do NOT proceed to commit/push

**4. First export:**
Skip verification when no baseline exists (previous commit has no file).

**Exit criteria:** All databases within threshold, or halted with escalation.

description:
Sync Dolt database backups to local and offsite storage.

The Backup Dog runs `dolt backup sync` for each production database, then
rsyncs the local backup directory to iCloud Drive for offsite replication.

Current behavior (from dolt_backup.go):
- Discovers databases with backup remotes configured
- Runs `dolt backup sync <name>-backup` per database
- Rsyncs .dolt-backup/ to iCloud Drive

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Sync each production database to its backup remote
2. Rsync local backups to offsite (iCloud Drive)
3. Report results to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| databases | config | List of databases to back up (or auto-discover) |

## Safety

Backups are additive — they never modify production data. Backup sync
overwrites the backup destination, but that is the intended behavior.

connectivity, latency, connection count, disk usage, database count (orphan
detection), and backup freshness.

Current behavior (from dolt.go checkHealthLocked):
1. Connectivity + latency: SELECT active_branch() with timing
2. Connection count: warn if approaching limit (80% of 50)
3. Disk usage: warn if data dir exceeds threshold
4. Database count: detect orphan test databases
5. Backup freshness: warn if backups are stale

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure work. You:
1. Probe Dolt server connectivity and latency
2. Inspect resource conditions (connections, disk, orphans)
3. Report findings to Deacon
4. Return to kennel

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |
| latency_threshold | config | Latency warning threshold (default 1s) |

## Safety

All checks are read-only. The Doctor Dog never modifies data.
It only observes and reports.

title: mol-dog-doctor

description:
Check resource usage and detect anomalies.

**1. Connection count:**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST;
```
Warn if >= 80% of max connections (1000).

**2. Disk usage (.dolt-data/):**
Check data directory size. Warn if exceeding configured threshold.

**3. Daemon directory disk usage:**
Check total daemon/ directory size:
```bash
du -sh <townRoot>/daemon/
```
Warn if > 500MB (the disk budget threshold). The Go-side `CleanDaemonDir()` runs
on heartbeat to enforce this, but report the current state for visibility.

**4. Database count (orphan detection):**
```sql
SHOW DATABASES;
```
Count databases matching orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, doctest_*.
If orphan count > threshold, recommend cleanup:
```bash
gt dolt cleanup
```

**5. Backup freshness:**
Check modification time of backup files. Warn if backups are stale
(older than 2x the configured backup interval).

**6. Record all findings:**
- Connection count and utilization percentage
- Data directory disk usage
- Daemon directory disk usage (warn if > 500MB)
- Orphan database count and names
- Backup freshness per database

**Exit criteria:** All inspections complete.
title: Inspect resource conditions

description:
Check basic server health and measure latency.

**1. Connectivity check:**
```bash
dolt sql -q "SELECT active_branch()"
```
Use active_branch() — lightweight probe that won't block behind queued queries
(per Dolt CEO recommendation).

**2. Measure latency:**
Time the probe query. Warn if > 5000ms.

**3. Record results:**
- Server reachable: yes/no
- Latency: <duration>
- Warning: if latency exceeds threshold

**If server unreachable:**
This is a critical failure. Escalate immediately:
```bash
gt escalate -s CRITICAL "Dolt: server unreachable on port 3307"
```

**Exit criteria:** Server connectivity confirmed (or escalated).
title: Probe Dolt server connectivity

description:
Stage, commit, and push JSONL files to the archive repository.

**1. Stage changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> add -A *.jsonl */
```

**2. Check for changes:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> diff --cached --quiet
# If no changes, skip commit
```

**3. Commit with counts:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> commit -m "backup <timestamp>: <db>=<count> ..." --author="Gas Town Daemon <daemon@gastown.local>"
```

Include failed databases in commit message so staleness is visible.

**4. Push to origin:**
```bash
git -C <git_repo> push origin main
```

**5. Handle push failures:**
Track consecutive failures. After 3 consecutive failures,
escalate to Mayor.

**Exit criteria:** Changes committed and pushed (or no changes to commit).
title: Commit and push to git archive

description:
Reap stale wisps and close stale issues across all Dolt databases.

The Reaper Dog executes this formula to clean up wisp and issue tables.
SQL operations are exposed via `gt reaper` CLI helpers — the Dog calls
these commands, inspects results, and uses judgment for anomalies.

## Dog Contract

This is infrastructure cleanup work:
1. Scan all production databases for candidates
2. Reap (close) wisps past max_age whose parent is closed/missing
3. Purge (delete) closed wisps past purge_age + old closed mail
4. Auto-close stale issues (>stale_issue_age, not P0/P1, not epics, no active deps)
5. Report findings and flag anomalies

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| max_age | config | Max wisp age before reaping (default 24h) |
| purge_age | config | Max closed wisp age before purging (default 7d) |
| stale_issue_age | config | Max issue staleness before auto-close (default 30d) |
| mail_delete_age | config | Max closed mail age before purging (default 7d) |
| alert_threshold | config | Open wisp count that triggers escalation (default 500) |
| dry_run | config | If "true", report without acting |
| databases | config | Comma-separated DB list (default: auto-discover) |
| dolt_port | config | Dolt server port (default 3307) |

## Safety

Reaping closes wisps — reversible (can reopen). Purging deletes rows — irreversible
but only targets already-closed wisps past retention. Auto-close only targets
issues that are not P0/P1, not epics, and have no active dependencies.

## Anomaly Detection

The Dog should watch for and flag:
- Dangling parent references (wisps referencing purged parents)
- Sudden spikes in reap candidates (suggests a wisp lifecycle problem)
- Open wisp counts exceeding alert_threshold
- Dolt commit failures (data may not be persisted)
title: mol-dog-reaper
